006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

017package org.apache.juneau.commons.bean;
018
019import java.util.*;
020/**
021 * Minimum runtime surface that the bean-modeling layer needs from a marshalling session.
022 *
023 * <p>
024 * {@code BeanSession} is an SPI seam between the bean-modeling runtime (in {@code commons.bean}) and the
025 * marshalling stack (in {@code juneau-marshall}).  The marshalling layer's {@code MarshallingSession}
026 * implements this interface so the bean-modeling types (notably {@code BeanMap} and {@code BeanPropertyMeta})
027 * can request session-aware operations — type conversion, bean-map wrapping — without referencing any
028 * marshalling-aware concrete types.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * On the commons-side construction path ({@code BeanMeta.of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}) the session is
032 * <jk>null</jk> and these operations are skipped; the bean-modeling code runs in raw-reflection mode.
033 *
034 * <h5 class='section'>Method parameter typing:</h5>
035 * <p>
036 * The {@code targetType} parameters are typed as {@link Object} because the marshalling layer expresses them
037 * as {@code ClassMeta<?>} — a marshalling-side type that {@code commons.bean} cannot reference.  Callers in
038 * the bean-modeling layer pass through whatever opaque type-metadata handle they received; the
039 * {@code MarshallingSession} implementation interprets it as a {@code ClassMeta}.
040 *
041 * <h5 class='topic'>Thread safety</h5>
042 * Thread safety depends on implementation.
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
045 *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfigContext} — bean-modeling configuration counterpart.
046 * </ul>
047 */
048public interface BeanSession {
049
050   /**
051    * Converts the specified value to the specified target type.
052    *
053    * <p>
054    * Implementations apply any registered conversions, swaps, or coercions necessary to produce a value
055    * compatible with {@code targetType}.  On the marshalling-side, this routes through
056    * {@code MarshallingSession.convertToType(Object, ClassMeta)}.
057    *
058    * @param value The value to convert.  May be <jk>null</jk>.
059    * @param targetType
060    *    The target type — typically a marshalling-side {@code ClassMeta<?>} handle passed through from
061    *    a {@code BeanPropertyMeta} or {@code BeanMap}.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
062    * @return The converted value.
063    */
064   Object convertToType(Object value, Object targetType);
065
066   /**
067    * Converts the specified value to the specified target type, instantiating non-static inner classes against
068    * an outer instance.
069    *
070    * @param outer The outer-class instance for non-static inner classes.  May be <jk>null</jk>.
071    * @param value The value to convert.  May be <jk>null</jk>.
072    * @param targetType The target type — typically a marshalling-side {@code ClassMeta<?>}.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
073    * @return The converted value.
074    */
075   Object convertToMemberType(Object outer, Object value, Object targetType);
076
077   /**
078    * Parses the specified JSON-formatted character sequence into a {@link Map}.
079    *
080    * <p>
081    * Used by {@code BeanPropertyMeta.setPropertyValue} when a {@link CharSequence} value is supplied for a
082    * {@code Map}-typed property — the bean-modeling layer cannot reference the marshalling-side JSON parser
083    * directly, so the parse is delegated to the session via this SPI.
084    *
085    * <p>
086    * Implementations typically route through {@code JsonMap.ofString(value).session(this)}.
087    *
088    * @param value The JSON-formatted character sequence to parse.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
089    * @return The parsed map.
090    */
091   @SuppressWarnings({
092      "java:S1452" // Map<?,?> wildcard return intentional; the key/value types are determined by the JSON content at runtime
093   })
094   Map<?,?> parseToMap(CharSequence value);
095
096   /**
097    * Parses the specified JSON-formatted character sequence into a {@link Collection}.
098    *
099    * <p>
100    * Used by {@code BeanPropertyMeta.setPropertyValue} when a {@link CharSequence} value is supplied for a
101    * {@code Collection}-typed property — the bean-modeling layer cannot reference the marshalling-side JSON
102    * parser directly, so the parse is delegated to the session via this SPI.
103    *
104    * <p>
105    * Implementations typically route through {@code new JsonList(value).setBeanSession(this)}.
106    *
107    * @param value The JSON-formatted character sequence to parse.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
108    * @return The parsed collection.
109    */
110   @SuppressWarnings({
111      "java:S1452" // Collection<?> wildcard return intentional; the element type is determined by the JSON content at runtime
112   })
113   Collection<?> parseToList(CharSequence value);
114
115   /**
116    * Wraps the specified bean in a {@code BeanMap}.
117    *
118    * <p>
119    * Equivalent to {@code MarshallingSession.toBeanMap(Object)} on the marshalling-side.  The return type is
120    * typed as {@link Object} (rather than {@code BeanMap}) because the bean-modeling {@code BeanMap} runtime
121    * type currently lives in {@code juneau-marshall}; callers in {@code commons.bean} treat the returned value
122    * opaquely and pass it through to other {@code BeanSession} / bean-modeling APIs that re-narrow it.
123    *
124    * <p>
125    * The signature is generic to allow implementations (notably {@code MarshallingSession}) to satisfy this
126    * method with their existing covariant {@code <T> BeanMap<T> toBeanMap(T)} declaration without an
127    * additional bridge method.
128    *
129    * @param <T> The bean type.
130    * @param bean The bean to wrap.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
131    * @return A new bean map (typically a {@code BeanMap}) wrapping the supplied bean.
132    */
133   <T> Object toBeanMap(T bean);
134
135   /**
136    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified bean property is visible under the current active view.
137    *
138    * <p>
139    * When no active view is set on the session, this method always returns <jk>true</jk> (all properties visible).
140    * The marshalling-side {@code MarshallingSession} overrides this to implement the actual view-membership check
141    * using the property's declared view set and the active view name.
142    *
143    * <p>
144    * Default-view-inclusion policy (configurable): an untagged property — one whose view set is empty — is
145    * considered in-view under every active view unless the
146    * {@code MarshallingContext.Builder.disableDefaultViewInclusion()} flag is set.
147    *
148    * @param pMeta The property metadata. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
149    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the property should be included under the current active view.
150    */
151   default boolean isPropertyInActiveView(BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) {
152      return true; // HTT — default SPI method; MarshallingSession always overrides, so this body is unreachable in practice.
153   }
154}
